title: Astene

infobox.value: * [Bachte](/w/index.php?title=Bachte&action=edit&redlink=1 "Bachte (page does not exist)") [[nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bachte "nl:Bachte")] * [Sint-Maria-Leerne](/w/index.php?title=Sint-Maria-Leerne&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sint-Maria-Leerne (page does not exist)") [[nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sint-Maria-Leerne "nl:Sint-Maria-Leerne")]

is a village and *[deelgemeente](/wiki/Deelgemeente "Deelgemeente")* (sub-municipality) in the municipality of [Deinze](/wiki/Deinze "Deinze") in the [Belgian](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium") province of [East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"). The village is located along the [Leie](/wiki/Leie "Leie") River and about 13 kilometres (8.1 mi) south-west of [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

["Parochiekerk Sint-Amandus en Sint-Job"](https://inventaris.onroerenderfgoed.be/erfgoedobjecten/38066). *Agentschap Onroerend Erfgoed* (in Dutch). Retrieved 8 May 2022.

**Astene** is a village and *[deelgemeente](/wiki/Deelgemeente "Deelgemeente")* (sub-municipality) in the municipality of [Deinze](/wiki/Deinze "Deinze") in the [Belgian](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium") province of [East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"). The village is located along the [Leie](/wiki/Leie "Leie") River and about 13 kilometres (8.1 mi) south-west of [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Astene&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The village was first mentioned in the 7th century as Athenneria. The etymology is unclear. The area has been inhabited since the [Roman era](/wiki/Roman_Empire "Roman Empire"), and graves has been discovered dating between 50 and 150 [AD](/wiki/Anno_Domini "Anno Domini"). The old parish church was located on the Kapelleberg, a dune near the [Leie](/wiki/Leie "Leie") River, and was first mentioned in 1147. The village *[heerlijkheid](/wiki/Heerlijkheid "Heerlijkheid")* (landed estate) used to belong to the [Margraviate](/wiki/Margraviate "Margraviate") of [Deinze](/wiki/Deinze "Deinze"), but the area also contained several other smaller *heerlijkheden*. In 1582, during the [Dutch Revolt](/wiki/Dutch_Revolt "Dutch Revolt"), the village was destroyed except for three houses and the area was nearly depopulated.[[3]](#cite_note-gesch-3)[[4]](#cite_note-invent-4)

Around 1720, the road from [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent") to [Kortrijk](/wiki/Kortrijk "Kortrijk") was constructed, and the centre moved from near the river to a [linear settlement](/wiki/Linear_settlement "Linear settlement") along the road. In 1834, a new parish church was built along the road.[[3]](#cite_note-gesch-3)[[4]](#cite_note-invent-4) In 1838, a railway station opened on the [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent") to [Mouscron](/wiki/Mouscron "Mouscron") (border with France) railway line,[[5]](#cite_note-5) and the village started to industrialise. There are several bunkers in the area from the 1930s which were constructed as part of a defensive line between the [Scheldt](/wiki/Scheldt "Scheldt") and the Leie. After World War II, Astene started to become a residential town.[[4]](#cite_note-invent-4)

Astene was an independent municipality until 1971, when it was merged to [Deinze](/wiki/Deinze "Deinze").[[3]](#cite_note-gesch-3)

## Buildings

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Astene&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Buildings")]

The St Amandus and St Job Church was built between 1834 and 1836 to replace the old and dilapidated parish church. The old church was demolished in 1836. The new church is a three aisled basilica-like church which was extended in 1878. During World War I, the tower was blown up and the church was severely damaged. It was restored and rebuilt in 1920. During World War II, it was again severely damaged. The church was restored and a new tower was built between 1948 and 1954.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Astene&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Notable people")]

* [Emile Claus](/wiki/Emile_Claus "Emile Claus") (1849–1924), painter[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* [Stéphan Martens](/wiki/St%C3%A9phan_Martens "Stéphan Martens") (born 1931), cyclist[[8]](#cite_note-8)
